description_long: This is a rustic, three-legged plant pot made from unglazed, weathered ceramic material. The pot has a rounded, bulbous body with a wide open mouth and a deep interior cavity, suitable for holding soil and a medium-sized plant. Its surface is mottled with earthy brown and dark gray tones, showing signs of age, wear, and some mineral deposits or stains. The three legs are thick and slightly uneven, giving the pot a sturdy stance and a primitive, hand-crafted appearance. There are no visible handles or decorative motifs, and the rim is slightly irregular, further emphasizing its artisanal quality. The overall look suggests it is robust and functional, ideal for outdoor or garden use.
description: A weathered, three-legged ceramic plant pot with a wide opening and rustic, earthy tones. Its surface is mottled and aged, giving it a hand-crafted, sturdy appearance.
description_view_0: The pot's rounded body and two of its three legs are clearly visible, with a wide open mouth and a mottled brown surface.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the pot's third leg and shows the slightly uneven rim and more of the pot's side curvature.
description_view_2: The view focuses on one leg and reveals mineral stains and surface wear on the upper right side of the pot.
description_view_3: The pot's body and all three legs are visible, with the most pronounced surface discoloration and weathering on the left side.
attribute_placement_context: commonly used in rustic outdoor gardens or patios

license_info.creator_display_name: The Royal Museums of Art and History
